Interface AuditRepository

All Known Implementing Classes:
CsvAuditRepository
public interface AuditRepository
Defines persistence operations for audit log entries across all service operations. Implementations store entries durably so the audit trail survives beyond a single command execution. The target object is stored as its string representation, since typed reconstruction is not required for audit display purposes.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    append(String staffId, String staffName, String action, String targetDescription, LocalDateTime timestamp)
    Appends a new audit log entry to the persistent store.
    void
    clear()
    Removes all audit entries from the store while preserving the file and its header.
    List<AuditEntry<String>>
    findAll()
    Returns all audit log entries ever persisted, ordered by timestamp ascending.

  • Method Details

    • append

      void append(String staffId, String staffName, String action, String targetDescription, LocalDateTime timestamp)
      Appends a new audit log entry to the persistent store. All parameters are required; no argument may be null or blank.
      Parameters:
      staffId - the unique identifier of the staff member who performed the action
      staffName - the display name of the staff member
      action - a short description of the action performed (e.g. "approved adoption")
      targetDescription - the string representation of the target object acted upon
      timestamp - the date and time the action occurred
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if any string argument is null or blank, or timestamp is null

    • findAll

      List<AuditEntry<String>> findAll()
      Returns all audit log entries ever persisted, ordered by timestamp ascending. Returns an empty list if no entries have been recorded. The target field of each returned entry contains the stored string description.
      Returns:
      a list of all audit entries with string-typed targets

    • clear

      void clear()
      Removes all audit entries from the store while preserving the file and its header. Intended for development and testing use only; do not call in production workflows.
